0.q: the plants overexpressing BADH displayed a 6- to 30-fold increase in betaine contents... These results indicate that the transformed BADH gene is functional and underpins the observed betaine accumulation under salt stress.
betaine aldehyde dehydrogenase (BADH) is a key enzyme involved in the biosynthesis of the osmoprotectant, glycine betaine (GB), and osmotic balance in plants
BADH activity in transgenic plants was more than 80 u, but was zero in controls... increasing of BADH activity can also improved the salinity tolerance of transgenic plants.

1.q: In higher plants, the pathway is the same, with choline monooxygenase (CMO) catalyzing the first step instead of CDH. BADH gene has been cloned from many organisms including spinach, Atriplex hortensis and wheat.
In plants, betaine aldehyde dehydrogenase (BADH) is a key enzyme in a two-step oxidation for GB synthesis.
Betaine aldehyde dehydrogenase (BADH) is the second enzyme involved in betaine biosynthesis.

3.q: Choline monooxygenase (CMO) catalyzes the committed step of glycine betaine (GlyBet) biosynthesis in many flowering plants.

4.q: high AhCMO transcription can enrich high levels of GlyBet; Over-expression of AhCMO improved drought tolerance in transgenic tobacco
